message:
[P2] fcp-mesh/node: ignore caller-supplied is_authenticated in handle_symbol_request; server-side state only

Closes flywheel_connectors-q92i7.

`MeshNode::handle_symbol_request` and its internal
`validate_symbol_request` previously did:

    let mut authenticated = is_authenticated || self.is_peer_authenticated(peer);

The OR construction meant that a caller passing `is_authenticated: true`
could elevate any peer past the unauthenticated tier regardless of
whether the peer held a real `MeshSession` or had been marked via
`admission.set_authenticated`. The result was threaded into:

  * `AdmissionController::check_admission` — skips the
    `AuthenticationRequired` reject (admission.rs:935-943) and uses the
    larger `authenticated_byte_limit` / `authenticated_symbol_limit`
    tiers (admission.rs:969-970).
  * The strict per-request unauthenticated-tier cap in
    `symbol_request.rs:287`.
  * `admission.set_authenticated(peer, authenticated, now_ms)` at
    node.rs:1172-1173 — which PERMANENTLY upgrades the peer's
    authentication bit (admission.rs:977-983) so every subsequent
    request inherits the elevated tier even when the caller stops
    passing `true`. One forged call, the peer is elevated forever.

The only production callers of `handle_symbol_request` are inside
tests — but the API shape invited a future transport layer or
embedding to pass a wire-supplied bool straight through, at which
point any peer holding an unverified session cookie becomes a
full-tier client. Closing the bypass at the boundary eliminates the
entire class.

Fix: `validate_symbol_request` now derives `authenticated` solely
from `self.is_peer_authenticated(peer)` (which consults
`self.sessions` + `self.admission`). The `is_authenticated`
parameter on `handle_symbol_request` is retained for ABI continuity
(renamed `_is_authenticated` to silence the unused-arg warning) and
documented as ignored — follow-up work can remove it once every
call site has migrated. `validate_symbol_request` itself drops the
parameter entirely since it is file-private.

Test updates:
  * `node::tests::prune_stale_state_clears_transfer_tracking` —
    added explicit `node.admission_mut().set_authenticated(&peer,
    true, 0)` before the request. This test had been relying on the
    OR bypass to reach the authenticated tier on a fresh node; the
    explicit pre-auth documents the intent.
  * New `node::tests::handle_symbol_request_ignores_caller_authenticated_flag`
    — regression test. Builds a node with no admission state, no
    sessions, no signed request; calls
    `handle_symbol_request(req, &peer, true, 0)` and asserts:
      (a) the call is rejected with `AuthenticationRequired` (or a
          signature-invalid variant if the signature fallback path
          fires), and
      (b) after the rejected call the peer is STILL
          unauthenticated (`node.is_peer_authenticated(&peer)` is
          `false`) — i.e., the forged bit did not persist via
          `admission.set_authenticated`.

Integration tests in `crates/fcp-mesh/tests/mesh_integration.rs`
continue to pass `true` as the third arg; the value is ignored. Any
integration test that was relying on the bypass would now hit the
unauthenticated tier, but in practice the integration fixtures
either set up real admission/session state or test error paths that
reject regardless of tier, so none flipped.

Verification:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p7-t14 cargo test -p fcp-mesh
    → 1185 passed; 2 failed (coordinator fencing-token tests,
      pre-existing and out of scope — same failures noted in kzrfp
      commit dfe0354a and opoux commit 60b57218).
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p7-t14 cargo test -p fcp-mesh --lib -- handle_symbol_request_ignores_caller_authenticated_flag prune_stale_state_clears_transfer_tracking
    → 2 passed; 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
